# UC-14.3.9 · Cold Storage Room Temperature Excursion Alert

## Description

Ensures pharmaceutical, food, or vaccine storage integrity by alerting within minutes of unplanned temperature rise.

## Value

Ensures pharmaceutical, food, or vaccine storage integrity by alerting within minutes of unplanned temperature rise.

## Implementation

Configure temperature sensor with Advanced Settings → Alerts enabled at -15°C upper threshold. Store locally for 30 minutes via SQLite backlog. For sub-zero operation, verify Edge Hub -40°C to 80°C operating range covers your environment. MQTT topic subscription can include external low-cost temp probes via I²C port (3.5mm jack).

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (temperature sensor ±0.2°C), Splunk OT Intelligence.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=temperature, `sourcetype=edge_hub`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Configure temperature sensor with Advanced Settings → Alerts enabled at -15°C upper threshold. Store locally for 30 minutes via SQLite backlog. For sub-zero operation, verify Edge Hub -40°C to 80°C operating range covers your environment. MQTT topic subscription can include external low-cost temp probes via I²C port (3.5mm jack).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| mstats avg(temperature) as temp by host, _time span=5m
| eval expected_range="[-20,-15]"
| where temp > -15 OR temp < -20
| eval deviation=if(temp > -15, temp - (-15), (-20) - temp)
| stats count as excursion_count, max(deviation) as max_deviation by host
| where excursion_count >= 3
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cold Storage Room Temperature Excursion Alert** — Ensures pharmaceutical, food, or vaccine storage integrity by alerting within minutes of unplanned temperature rise.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=temperature, `sourcetype=edge_hub`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (temperature sensor ±0.2°C), Splunk OT Intelligence. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `mstats` to query metrics indexes (pre-aggregated metric data).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **expected_range** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where temp > -15 OR temp < -20` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **deviation** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where excursion_count >= 3` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Note: for mstats, confirm the metrics indexes in the search exist and your role can run mstats. This is separate from CIM data model acceleration.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm metric or event volume in the time range, then compare trends and device status to the Splunk Edge Hub admin UI or the mobile app for the same devices. Verify metric names, hosts, and time skew.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single-value alert indicator, time-series trend, deviation log.

## SPL

```spl
| mstats avg(temperature) as temp by host, _time span=5m
| eval expected_range="[-20,-15]"
| where temp > -15 OR temp < -20
| eval deviation=if(temp > -15, temp - (-15), (-20) - temp)
| stats count as excursion_count, max(deviation) as max_deviation by host
| where excursion_count >= 3
```

## Visualization

Single-value alert indicator, time-series trend, deviation log.
